BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick check on whether the editing/tutoring side gig covers its software subscriptions and adds anything to the buffer during thesis crunch.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate; income is seasonal and clusters around semester starts. Software line is the fixed floor the side gig has to clear before anything hits savings. The rent/utilities/insurance/cleaning/instructor lines are zero because Alex is a grad student on a side gig, not running a studio.

Scenario_A_OneClientMonth.Label: Editing client volume drops to one per month during thesis crunch
Scenario_A_OneClientMonth.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed software ~$70/mo. At ~$85 per editing client, one client per month covers the software floor with a small margin.
Scenario_A_OneClientMonth.Impact: Still net-positive at one client a month; loan draw pauses that month.

Scenario_B_AddWeeklyTutoring.Label: Add one weekly tutoring student
Scenario_B_AddWeeklyTutoring.Impact: Meaningfully boosts the buffer, but only viable in weeks that are not carrying a thesis deadline.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: N/A for Alex (no studio rent)
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: N/A for Alex; no membership-dues revenue line and no studio rent line to solve against.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Not applicable; this scenario belongs to a studio-owner variant of the template and is retained only for schema parity.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: N/A for Alex (no studio rent)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Not applicable; this scenario belongs to a studio-owner variant of the template and is retained only for schema parity.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is about one editing client per month. Everything above that goes to the loan-payment buffer and to the visit-weekend fund.
ActionItems: Cap tutoring hours during the October chapter deadline window
Revisit pricing in January once thesis load eases
